data.title: As a developer, sprint and sub-agents record their active branch on each ticket via WORKTREE_TRACKING:start comments
data.description:
## Description

**What**: Adds WORKTREE_TRACKING:start comment writes to the beginning of task-level sub-agent work and to orchestrator session start (sprint/fix-bug). Produces a format specification for all WORKTREE_TRACKING comment types that downstream stories (B and C) must follow.

**Why**: Creates the data source and the shared comment format contract for the resume recovery logic — without these comments and a stable format spec, there is no branch record to scan on resume, and downstream implementations risk incompatible formats.

**Scope**:
- IN: Writing WORKTREE_TRACKING:start comment to task tickets when implementation sub-agents begin work (via task-execution.md template update); writing WORKTREE_TRACKING:start comment to epic/bug tickets when sprint/fix-bug begins a session; fail-silent behavior when .tickets-tracker/ is unavailable; authoring the WORKTREE_TRACKING comment format specification covering all three comment types (:start, :complete, :landed)
- OUT: Detecting or acting on existing start comments (Story C); writing :complete/:landed comments (Story B)

## Done Definitions

- When this story is complete, a WORKTREE_TRACKING comment format specification is defined (covering :start, :complete, and :landed comment types — field names, serialization, and an example for each) and is referenced by Stories B and C as the authoritative format contract
  ← Satisfies: "When an implementation sub-agent begins work on a task ticket, it posts a WORKTREE_TRACKING:start comment recording its branch name and the orchestrator's session branch before any implementation begins." (format contract prerequisite)

- When this story is complete, any implementation sub-agent dispatched by sprint or fix-bug writes a WORKTREE_TRACKING:start comment on its task ticket before beginning implementation, including the worktree branch name and orchestrator session branch conforming to the format spec, and the write fails silently if .tickets-tracker/ is unavailable
  ← Satisfies: "When an implementation sub-agent begins work on a task ticket, it posts a WORKTREE_TRACKING:start comment recording its branch name and the orchestrator's session branch before any implementation begins."

- When this story is complete, sprint writes a WORKTREE_TRACKING:start comment on the epic ticket in Phase 1 after ticket validation, and fix-bug writes a WORKTREE_TRACKING:start comment on the bug ticket in Step 0.5 after the ticket is set to in_progress; both obtain the session branch name via `git branch --show-current`
  ← Satisfies: "When sprint or fix-bug begins a session on an epic or bug ticket, it posts a WORKTREE_TRACKING:start comment recording the session branch before dispatching sub-agents."

- When this story is complete, when .tickets-tracker/ is unavailable, WORKTREE_TRACKING:start writes fail silently without blocking sub-agent dispatch or orchestrator startup
  ← Satisfies: "When ticket comment writes fail (e.g., .tickets-tracker/ unavailable), WORKTREE_TRACKING writes fail silently without blocking sub-agent work."

- When this story is complete, unit tests are written and passing for all new or modified logic

## Considerations

- [Implementation] Sub-agent :start writes require modifying the task-execution.md prompt template (plugins/dso/skills/sprint/prompts/task-execution.md) to inject the write before CHECKPOINT 1/6; the template must receive the worktree branch name as a new template variable injected by the orchestrator in the sub-agent dispatch prompt
- [Maintainability] WORKTREE_TRACKING:start writes span sprint SKILL.md, fix-bug SKILL.md, and task-execution.md — the format spec deliverable is the mechanism to prevent format drift across all three callers
- [Reliability] :start writes are new side-effects in sub-agent dispatch and orchestrator startup — if .tickets-tracker/ is detached or read-only in isolated worktrees, writes must fail silently without aborting dispatch

## Escalation Policy

**Escalation policy**: Proceed unless a significant assumption is required to continue — one that could send the implementation in the wrong direction. Escalate only when genuinely blocked without a reasonable inference. Document all assumptions made without escalating.
